sales.description:
Ancients
Gaius (Caligula) (AD 37-41). AR denarius (19mm, 3.53 gm, 4h). NGC Choice XF 5/5 - 4/5. Rome, AD 37-38. C CAESAR•AVG•GERM•P•M•TR•POT, laureate head of Caligula right / GERMANICVS•CAES•P•C•CAES•AVG•GERM, bare head of Germanicus right. RIC I 18. Gunmetal toning and a firm strike accent the devices.

Ex Dr. Patrick Tan Collection (Goldberg, Auction 98, 6 June 2017), lot 2229; Nomos, Auction 11 (9 October 2015), lot 164.

Germanicus, the father of Gaius, was the John F. Kennedy of his gene...

sales.description:
Ancients
Augustus (27 BC-AD 14). AR denarius (17mm, 3.80 gm, 2h). NGC VF 4/5 - 3/5. Lugdunum, 8 BC. AVGVSTVS-DIVI•F, laureate head of Augustus right / C•CAES / AVGVS•F, Gaius Caesar on horse leaping right, reins in right hand, sword and shield in left; aquila right between two standards beyond. RIC I 199.

From The Peh Family Collection, Part IV. Ex Baldwin's, Auction XXV (5 January 2011), lot 49.

Tiberius (AD 14-37). AR denarius (18mm, 3.65 gm, 4h). NGC Choice AU 5/5 - 2/5. Lugdunum, ca. AD 18-35. TI CAESAR DIVI-AVG F AVGVSTVS, laureate head of Tiberius right / PONTIF-MAXIM, Livia, as
sales.description:
Ancients
Tiberius (AD 14-37). AR denarius (18mm, 3.65 gm, 4h). NGC Choice AU 5/5 - 2/5. Lugdunum, ca. AD 18-35. TI CAESAR DIVI-AVG F AVGVSTVS, laureate head of Tiberius right / PONTIF-MAXIM, Livia, as Pax, seated right, feet on stool, grounded scepter in right hand, olive branch in outstretched left; chair with ornate legs, single line below. RIC I 30.
